author: @Donnis32
text: In case someone hasn't answered earlier; with core XY printers, the print head moves on X and Y and is stationary on the Z axis (usually). The bed is stationary on the X and Y and only moves on the Z (usually). Some core XY printers have a completely stationary bed and the print heat moves on all three axis. In general, core XY are slightly more stable and can run at faster speeds; the trade off is that they tend to be more expensive and use more parts. The low end of "Ender" style printers in the US is around $160 - $180. The low end of core XY is in the $350 - $500 range. Both styles have advantages and disadvantages; and like much of the 3D printing hobby its very subjective to the users preferences.

author: @zylascope
text:
Awesome! The sound is good, I can hear you, Luke and your one true love :), but it sounded slightly 'low-res' mp3 audio in places, mainly the first few minutes, I noticed that the sound improved throughout the video, not sure why or how. Were you wearing a mic? Or was the mic near the camera? That's probably all that caused it. 

Elegoo made a great move by sending some of their 3D printers to you Rob, someone who'll put them to the test and drive them to their limit! I love what you've done with sharing your print designs. I'm having a great night in the lab (kitchen) watching this. Thanks Rob, Luke, and Patty! 

Edit: Watching this has given me a major upgrade to my sense of humor! Gee I would love to work at T'n'T. I'm still going to visit Canterbury one day and play Pink Floyd at the Christmas party with Rob on Ukulele!!!
